description: A designed humor/propaganda meme styled as a handwritten doctor's prescription (Rx) pad on a plain white background. In the upper-left is a large bold serif 'Rx' symbol. To its right, in printed type, 'patient info:' above three underlined handwritten lines reading 'TRUMP DERANGEMENT SYNDROME'. A double horizontal rule separates the header from the body. Below, in handwritten-style block lettering, is 'Treatment -' followed by a bulleted list: 'TRUSTING IN TRUMP', 'LISTEN TO NATIONAL ANTHEM', 'LIMIT FAKE NEWS', and "DON'T BE A PANICAN". At the bottom are handwritten fields 'date: MAY 11' and 'signature:' with a scribbled signature. In the top-right corner is the small engraved White House building logo. Visual style: minimalist black-on-white mock medical form imitating a real prescription. Messaging/propaganda technique: political satire that frames criticism of Trump as a fake mental illness ('Trump Derangement Syndrome') and prescribes loyalty, patriotism, distrust of mainstream media ('FAKE NEWS'), and not panicking ('PANICAN', an in-group epithet for those who panic over Trump's policies) as the 'cure'. It is a shareable in-joke ('Send to someone who needs', per the tweet). Target audience: Trump supporters and online partisans, meant to mock critics and reinforce loyalty/calm.
notable_text: Rx patient info: TRUMP DERANGEMENT SYNDROME | Treatment - TRUSTING IN TRUMP / LISTEN TO NATIONAL ANTHEM / LIMIT FAKE NEWS / DON'T BE A PANICAN | date: MAY 11
